excerpts.text: You have power over your mind, not outside events. Realize this, and you will find strength.
The impediment to action advances action. What stands in the way becomes the way.
Waste no more time arguing about what a good man should be. Be one.
Loss is nothing else but change, and change is Nature's delight.
The unexamined life is not worth living.
I know that I am intelligent, because I know that I know nothing.
Wonder is the beginning of wisdom.
At the touch of love, everyone becomes a poet.
Thinking is the talking of the soul with itself.
The price good men pay for indifference to public affairs is to be ruled by evil men.
Man is by nature a political animal.
Happiness is the meaning and the purpose of life, the whole aim and end of human existence.
It is the mark of an educated mind to be able to entertain a thought without accepting it.
That which does not kill us makes us stronger.
Without music, life would be a mistake.
He who has a why to live for can bear almost any how.
It is not a lack of love, but a lack of friendship that makes unhappy marriages.
Act only according to that maxim by which you can at the same time will that it should become a universal law.
excerpts.source: Groundwork of the Metaphysics of Morals

excerpts.text: Two things fill the mind with ever new and increasing admiration and awe: the starry heavens above me and the moral law within me.
Dare to know. Have the courage to use your own understanding.
Knowing others is intelligence; knowing yourself is true wisdom. Mastering others is strength; mastering yourself is true power.
A journey of a thousand miles begins with a single step.
Nature does not hurry, yet everything is accomplished.
It does not matter how slowly you go so long as you do not stop.
When you know a thing, to hold that you know it; and when you do not know a thing, to allow that you do not know it — this is knowledge.
He who learns but does not think is lost. He who thinks but does not learn is in great danger.
We suffer more in imagination than in reality.
excerpts.source: Letters from a Stoic, Letter LXXXV

excerpts.text: Luck is what happens when preparation meets opportunity.
excerpts.source: Letters from a Stoic, Letter LXXXVII

excerpts.text: Begin at once to live, and count each separate day as a separate life.
excerpts.source: Letters from a Stoic, Letter I

excerpts.text: It's not what happens to you, but how you react to it that matters.
Men are disturbed not by the things which happen, but by the opinions about the things.
First say to yourself what you would be; then do what you have to do.
Death does not concern us, because as long as we exist, death is not here. And when it does come, we no longer exist.
Do not spoil what you have by desiring what you have not; remember that what you now have was once among the things you only hoped for.
Of all the things which wisdom provides to make us entirely happy, much the greatest is the possession of friendship.
Talent hits a target no one else can hit; genius hits a target no one else can see.
excerpts.source: The World as Will and Representation

excerpts.text: The two enemies of human happiness are pain and boredom.
Compassion is the basis of morality.
Life can only be understood backwards; but it must be lived forwards.
The most common form of despair is not being who you are.
Anxiety is the dizziness of freedom.
In the midst of winter, I found there was, within me, an invincible summer.
One must imagine Sisyphus happy.
The struggle itself toward the heights is enough to fill a man's heart.
Existence precedes essence.
Man is condemned to be free.
Freedom is what you do with what's been done to you.
Reason is, and ought only to be, the slave of the passions.
excerpts.source: A Treatise of Human Nature, Book II

excerpts.text: Beauty in things exists in the mind which contemplates them.
Custom is the great guide of human life.
All things excellent are as difficult as they are rare.
I have made a ceaseless effort not to ridicule, not to bewail, not to scorn human actions, but to understand them.
The highest activity a human being can attain is learning for understanding, because to understand is to be free.
Nothing great in the world has been accomplished without passion.
excerpts.source: Lectures on the Philosophy of History

excerpts.text: The owl of Minerva spreads its wings only with the falling of the dusk.
We learn from history that we do not learn from history.
excerpts.source: Lectures on the Philosophy of History (attributed)

excerpts.text: I think, therefore I am.
excerpts.source: Discourse on the Method, Part IV

excerpts.text: It is not enough to have a good mind; the main thing is to use it well.
excerpts.source: Discourse on the Method, Part I

excerpts.text: Divide each difficulty into as many parts as is feasible and necessary to resolve it.
excerpts.source: Discourse on the Method, Part II

excerpts.text: Whereof one cannot speak, thereof one must be silent.
The limits of my language mean the limits of my world.
If a lion could speak, we could not understand him.
The heart has its reasons of which reason knows nothing.
All of humanity's problems stem from man's inability to sit quietly in a room alone.
Man is but a reed, the most feeble thing in nature; but he is a thinking reed.
Once upon a time, I dreamt I was a butterfly. Now I do not know whether I was then a man dreaming I was a butterfly, or whether I am now a butterfly dreaming I am a man.
Flow with whatever may happen and let your mind be free.
The fish trap exists because of the fish. Once you've gotten the fish, you can forget the trap.
Philosophers have hitherto only interpreted the world in various ways; the point is to change it.
The production of too many useful things results in too many useless people.
From each according to his ability, to each according to his needs.
Language is the house of Being. In its home man dwells.
Why are there beings at all instead of nothing?
Man acts as though he were the shaper and master of language, while in fact language remains the master of man.
The good life is one inspired by love and guided by knowledge.
The whole problem with the world is that fools and fanatics are always so certain of themselves, and wiser people so full of doubts.
In all affairs it is a healthy thing now and then to hang a question mark on the things you have long taken for granted.
The things that we love tell us what we are.
A small error in the beginning grows a thousandfold toward the end.
Love takes up where knowledge leaves off.
Change your life today. Don't gamble on the future, act now, without delay.
One is not born, but rather becomes, a woman.
I am incapable of conceiving infinity, and yet I do not accept finity.
The smallest act in the most limited circumstances bears the seed of the same boundlessness, because one deed, and sometimes one word, suffices to change every constellation.
Storytelling reveals meaning without committing the error of defining it.
The meaning of life is just to be alive. It is so plain and so obvious and so simple. And yet everybody rushes around in a great panic as if it were necessary to achieve something beyond themselves.
You are a function of what the whole universe is doing in the same way that a wave is a function of what the whole ocean is doing.
excerpts.source: The Book: On the Taboo Against Knowing Who You Are

excerpts.text: Attention is the rarest and purest form of generosity.
The love of our neighbour in all its fullness simply means being able to say to him: What are you going through?
Where there is power, there is resistance.
Is it surprising that prisons resemble factories, schools, barracks, hospitals, which all resemble prisons?

serious: The river does not fight the stone. The river becomes the stone's biography.
To seek the answer is already to have found the question. To find the question is to have misplaced your keys.
Silence speaks loudest in rooms where no one has listened.
The shadow you cast at noon is the you that noon refuses to acknowledge.
Every closed door is simply a wall that learned ambition.
The wound is not where the arrow landed, but where you were standing when it did.
We do not inherit the stars. We borrow them, briefly, and call it home.
What you resist does not persist — it simply waits in a different corridor of the self.
The tree does not apologize for the autumn. Neither should you.
To be lost is simply to be very, very present in a place no map has visited yet.
balanced: The universe noticed your question and immediately scheduled a meeting with itself.
Growth is just confusion that decided to stay for breakfast.
You are both the gardener and the somewhat stubborn weed. Negotiations are ongoing.
The answer you're looking for is probably behind the thing you've been avoiding — right next to your charger.
Every sunrise is the universe's way of saying: alright, let's try this again.
You are simultaneously too hard and too soft on yourself. Somewhere in the middle, there is soup.
The path forward is clear, if you squint a little and ignore that other path.
The cosmos is sending you a sign. It's just stuck in traffic.
Progress is just past mistakes wearing better shoes.
You already know what to do. You're just hoping the universe will do it for you. Relatable.
kardashian: The road you take is the road you're on.
When you have unanswered questions, you're someone who's questioning.
At the end of the day, the day has ended. And you were in it.
Your journey is uniquely yours, which is really just another way of saying that your journey is your journey.
Everything that happens is really just what's happening. When you sit with that, it's a lot.
The choices you made are what led you to where the choices you made have led you.
Growth is becoming more of what you're becoming. It just is.
If you're not where you want to be, then where you want to be is somewhere you're not. Right now.
Sometimes the hardest part of going through something is the part where you're in it.
The people in your life are in your life. The ones who left have left. And that, for me, is kind of everything.
The future hasn't happened yet, but when it does, it'll be the present. And you'll be there.
Everything you've been through has brought you to everything you've been through.
When something ends, it's over. Which, honestly, can feel like an ending.
Your truth is yours. That's what makes it not someone else's.
The direction you're heading is where you're going. You just have to keep going in that direction.
I've realized that the things I don't understand are the things I haven't understood yet. And that's actually really deep.
What you feel is a feeling you're having. And feelings are real because you're feeling them.
The version of you that exists right now is the you that exists at this moment in time.
When time passes, what was happening stops happening. And then something else starts happening, or nothing does.
We all have a story. And that story is really just everything that's happened to us, told in order.
